MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Campbell Construction Co.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-03-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT CAMPBELL CONSTRUCTION CO.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

APPEARANCES:
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff Douglas Bell.
Jason Kort of BERKES CRANE ROBINSON & SEAL LLP (213-955-1150), appearing for defendant Campbell Construction Company.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Campbell Construction Co.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff Douglas Bell does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) Defendant does not address Plaintiff's interrogatory responses identifying witnesses Pat Tillman and Robert Lee, or his deposition testimony identifying classmates Pat Tillman, Brenda and Tom Mock, Mel Friedman, and Lee Boek. (Defendant's Ex. B [Plaintiff's Supplemental/Amended Supplemental/Amended Responses to Standard Interrogatories] at 1-2; Plaintiff's Ex. A [Deposition Testimony of Douglas Bell] at 15-21; Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Constr. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 81 [the court "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].)

Order is signed in open court.

Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: S. Le. Reporter: Jennifer Roux of Aiken Welch, CSR #11033, jroux@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 877-451-1580). (503/CML)

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER ADVANCING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE; AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Hearing held, argued and the court rules as follows: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The January 21, 2020 trial date is advanced to October 7, 2019 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is October 18, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is October 3, 2019. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 20, 2019. Expert discovery cut-off date is October 4, 2019. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 19, 2019. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Gilliam F. Stewart of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Luppen and Hawley, Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Douglas Bell

RULING:
DEFENDANT LUPPEN AND HAWLEY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Luppen and Hawley, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to submit admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiff failed to submit admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether the employees who exposed plaintiff to asbestos-containing cement pipe at La Sierra High School between 1955-1959 were employed by defendant. Defendant's objections to the Division of the State Architect documents (Exhibit B) are sustained as inadmissible hearsay. This ruling makes defendant's motion on the issue of duty moot. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
